# Fleetwood Mac — Timeline

*Generated by `scripts/build_events.py`. Hand-entered events from `data/events.yaml` merged with events generated from album and tour records, sorted by date.*

Significance: ★ major · • notable · · minor


## 1967

- ★ **July 1967** — Peter Green forms Fleetwood Mac
- • **13 August 1967** — Fleetwood Mac play their first show at the Windsor Festival
- · **September 1967** — Bob Brunning steps aside for John McVie
- • **September 1967** — John McVie joins the band that already bears his name

## 1968

- • **1968** — John McVie marries Christine Perfect
- • **24 February 1968** — Fleetwood Mac release Fleetwood Mac _(generated)_
- • **August 1968** — Eighteen-year-old Danny Kirwan joins as third guitarist
- • **23 August 1968** — Fleetwood Mac release Mr. Wonderful _(generated)_

## 1969

- ★ **January 1969** — Instrumental "Albatross" reaches UK #1
- • **19 September 1969** — Fleetwood Mac release Then Play On _(generated)_

## 1970

- ★ **20 May 1970** — Peter Green leaves Fleetwood Mac
- ★ **1 August 1970** — Christine McVie joins Fleetwood Mac
- · **18 September 1970** — Fleetwood Mac release Kiln House _(generated)_

## 1971

- • **1971** — Bob Welch becomes Fleetwood Mac's first American member
- ★ **15 February 1971** — Jeremy Spencer disappears mid-tour to join the Children of God
- · **3 September 1971** — Fleetwood Mac release Future Games _(generated)_

## 1972

- · **1972** — Dave Walker and Bob Weston join the lineup
- · **March 1972** — Fleetwood Mac release Bare Trees _(generated)_
- • **August 1972** — Danny Kirwan is fired after a backstage breakdown

## 1973

- · **1973** — Dave Walker is asked to leave
- · **March 1973** — Fleetwood Mac release Penguin _(generated)_
- · **5 September 1973** — Buckingham Nicks release Buckingham Nicks _(generated)_
- • **October 1973** — Bob Weston is fired over his affair with Jenny Boyd Fleetwood
- · **15 October 1973** — Fleetwood Mac release Mystery to Me _(generated)_

## 1974

- ★ **January 1974** — A fake Fleetwood Mac tours the US
- • **13 September 1974** — Fleetwood Mac release Heroes Are Hard to Find _(generated)_
- • **December 1974** — Bob Welch leaves, exhausted by the lawsuits and turnover
- ★ **31 December 1974** — Lindsey Buckingham and Stevie Nicks join Fleetwood Mac

## 1975

- ★ **11 July 1975** — Fleetwood Mac release Fleetwood Mac _(generated)_

## 1976

- • **1976** — John and Christine McVie divorce
- ★ **4 September 1976** — Fleetwood Mac reaches US #1 over a year after release

## 1977

- ★ **4 February 1977** — Fleetwood Mac release Rumours _(generated)_
- ★ **24 February 1977** — Fleetwood Mac begin the Rumours Tour _(generated)_
- ★ **2 April 1977** — Rumours hits US #1 and stays there
- • **June 1977** — "Dreams" becomes the band's only US #1 single

## 1978

- ★ **23 February 1978** — Rumours wins the Grammy for Album of the Year
- ★ **30 August 1978** — The Rumours Tour comes to an end _(generated)_

## 1987

- ★ **8 August 1987** — Lindsey Buckingham quits rather than tour Tango in the Night

## 1991

- ★ **1991** — Stevie Nicks leaves Fleetwood Mac

## 1997

- ★ **1997** — Buckingham and Nicks rejoin Fleetwood Mac
- ★ **1997** — The classic lineup reunites for The Dance

## 1998

- • **1998** — Christine McVie retires from the band

## 2011

- · **18 October 2011** — Bob Brunning dies at 68

## 2012

- · **3 January 2012** — Bob Weston dies at 64
- • **7 June 2012** — Bob Welch dies at 66

## 2014

- • **January 2014** — Christine McVie rejoins after sixteen years away

## 2018

- ★ **January 2018** — Lindsey Buckingham is fired from Fleetwood Mac
- • **8 June 2018** — Danny Kirwan dies at 68

## 2020

- ★ **25 July 2020** — Peter Green dies at 73

## 2022

- ★ **30 November 2022** — Christine McVie dies at 79
